title: Mitrephora humilis
intro: Mitrephora humilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Mitrephora humilis include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Mitrephora humilis is often prepared into herbal infusions, teas, and decoctions, or made into capsules and tablets to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Mitrephora humilis belongs to the family Annonaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Mitrephora. The Annonaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard-apple or soursop family. This family consists of over 2,300 species of trees and shrubs. The plant is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia.
